title: Argument exceptions should name a real parameter
messageFormat: The parameter name passed to this argument exception does not match any parameter in scope.
summary: The paramName argument of ArgumentException and its relatives is what tooling, logs and callers use to find the offending input. When the literal passed there is misspelled or stale, the diagnostic points at a parameter that does not exist and the real culprit stays hidden.
Use nameof(parameter) so the compiler keeps the name correct through every rename.
